title: Glyoxal fixation of mammalian cells for immunofluorescence
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ctus","text":"This is our standard protocol for glyoxal fixation of mammalian cells grown on glass cover slips for immunofluorescence microscopy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Wash the cells with warm DMEM without FCS.
Add 300µl glyoxal solution per well (12-well plate) and incubate on ice for 30 minutes On ice 00:30:00 , then at room temperature for 20 minutes Room temperature 00:20:00 .
Add 300µl quenching solution for 20 minutes Room temperature 00:20:00
Wash once with PBS At this stage, fixed cells can be stored in the fridge for at least a week.

Add 500µl blocking solution and incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes Room temperature 00:30:00 In meantime, prepare the primary antibody. The dilution is dependent on the antibody, but can range from undiluted for non-purified hybridoma supernatants to 1:1000 for affinity purified antibodies. The antibodies are diluted in antibody buffer .

There are two ways to incubate the antibodies: 1. Prepare enough so that the slide can be covered in the well with the antibody solution, ±250-300µl. 2. To save antibody, prepare only 50µl and pipette this onto parafilm. Place the coverslip on top of this drop with the cells facing the drop and incubate in a humid chamber. Incubate at least 45 minutes at room temperature or alternatively overnight at 4ºC Room temperature 00:45:00
Place the coverslips back in the 12-well plate with the cells facing upwards . Wash 3 times with PBS.

Dilute the secondary antibody in antibody buffer , generally in a range of 1:1000-1:2000 for most antibodies. Add 250-300µl per well and incubate for at least 45 minutes at room temperature or alternatively overnight at 4ºC Room temperature 00:45:00 . IMPORTANT: this incubation step should be in the dark, as the conjugated secondary antibodies are light-sensitive.
Wash 2 times in PBS, followed by 2 times in ddH2O.

Place a drop of mountant containing DAPI on a microscope slide and place the coverslip on this drop with the cells facing the drop . IMPORTANT: warm up the mountant to 37ºC to avoid unequal DAPI-staining. Tip the coverslip sideways on a tissue to remove excess water before mounting.
Dry the slides overnight in a dark place at room temperature. Store at 4ºC afterwards until imaging.
creator: Karla LH Feijs
